bio.title: Sgt.
bio.bio_blurb: Bulge veteran turned Wisconsin bank president

military_service.notes: Battle of the Bulge; among the first across the Rhine at Remagen. Bronze Star and Purple Heart.

narrative_blocks.header: Bronze Star in the Bulge’s frozen foxholes
narrative_blocks.body: With the 309th Infantry of the 78th Division, Darwin Blanke spent the winter of 1944–45 in the foxholes of the Battle of the Bulge and was among the first across the Rhine at Remagen, earning the Bronze Star and Purple Heart.

narrative_blocks.header: He grew his bank sixfold as president
narrative_blocks.body: A banker for nearly forty years, he was named president of the First National Bank of Wisconsin Rapids in 1969 and saw its assets grow from twenty-two to a hundred and twenty million dollars.

career.organization: First National Bank of Wisconsin Rapids
career.notes: Nearly forty years a banker; assets grew from $22M to $120M under his presidency.

bio.bio_blurb: Medical technologist; daughter of a state justice
